type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The MacKay River Commercial Project (MRCP) is an oil sands facility which takes several years to ramp-up to full production. Throughout 2017 and 2018 production wells continue to be initiated and ramped up. Change in estimation methodology: PCC CEMS/engineering estimates used in place of stack test data.

comments.comment: There were more days with precipitation or snow cover in 2019 than 2018 at the Facility's location, as a result there was less road dust emissions due to natural mitigation.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Previously flare PM10 emissions were assumed to be negligible. For the 2019 NPRI, PM10 emissions from flaring were quantified with AERFLARE derived emission factors.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Previously flare PM2.5 emissions were assumed to be negligible. For the 2019 NPRI, P2.5 emissions from flaring were quantified with AERFLARE derived emission factors.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The AP-42 flare VOC emission factor was previously used to estimate VOC emissions from the facility's flare. For the 2019 NPRI, the flare VOC emission factor was changed to one derived using AERFLare. Emission factors derived using AERFlare are based on the facility's flare stack's parameters; therefore, they are likely more accurate than AP-42 emission factors.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Decrease in the amount of natural gas injected.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
There was an error in the mass balance formula used to calculate SO2 emissions from emergency flaring in 2019 which resulted in over estimating emissions. 

The composition of natural gas was also used to estimate routine flaring emissions instead of flare gas composition, which was used previously in 2019. Flare gas samples can be contaminated with H2S from process gases which are not routinely flared. All routine flaring streams are natural gas.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Flare gas composition data in 2019 was contaminated with H2S from process gasses, which are not routinely flared, as a result H2S emissions from routine flaring were over estimated in 2019. For the 2020 NPRI, routine flaring emissions are estimated using natural gas composition data, which is what is routinely used as the flare's assist, pilot and purge gas. Emissions are also down due to less gas being vented in 2020.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The control efficiency for road dust due to watering was reduced to 55% as the unpaved roads are not watered more than twice per day.
